claim: Agentic workflows (iterative loops with reflection, planning, tool use) significantly outperform zero-shot prompting - GPT-3.5 with agentic workflow beats GPT-4 zero-shot on coding benchmarks
actionable: Always wrap coding and reasoning tasks in agentic loops with reflection and iteration rather than relying on single model outputs
source_title: What's next for AI agentic workflows ft. Andrew Ng

claim: Reflection (having agents evaluate their own outputs and fix errors) is a robust, mature pattern that works reliably and should be used widely
actionable: Add self-reflection prompts where agents check their code/work for correctness and iteratively refine
source_title: What's next for AI agentic workflows ft. Andrew Ng

claim: Planning and multi-agent collaboration are emerging techniques that work well in demos but are not yet reliably deployable in production without careful tuning
actionable: Use reflection first (it works); test planning and multi-agent collaboration heavily before depending on them in production
source_title: What's next for AI agentic workflows ft. Andrew Ng

claim: Multi-agent systems (separate coder and critic agents) are more effective than single-agent workflows for code quality, even with the same underlying model
actionable: Use a two-agent pattern (coder + critic/reviewer) instead of asking a single agent to both write and review code
source_title: What's next for AI agentic workflows ft. Andrew Ng

claim: Users expect immediate responses from LLMs, but agentic workflows require patience for iterative loops that may take minutes or hours - this is a UX and behavior change needed for adoption
actionable: Set expectations with users that agentic workflows take time; design interfaces to show progress and allow agent delegation
source_title: What's next for AI agentic workflows ft. Andrew Ng

claim: Fast token generation from even slightly weaker models may outperform slower generation from stronger models in agentic workflows due to more iteration cycles
actionable: Consider speed trade-offs when choosing models for agentic loops - faster weaker models may deliver better results than slower stronger ones
source_title: What's next for AI agentic workflows ft. Andrew Ng
